---
title: "Roadmap"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.container}
# Roadmap

A structured view of the project: how the model components fit together and how users typically move from basic fits to advanced workflows. Use this page as the “map,” and the Cookbook as the “hands-on guide.”

[Go to Cookbook](cookbook.html){.btn .btn-primary}
[Browse Kernels](kernels.html){.btn .btn-outline-light}
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.container}

::: {.grid-3}

::: {.cardx}
### 1) Start small
Confirm the basic build and run cycle on a small dataset before scaling up.
- Entry: [Getting Started](getting-started.html)
:::

::: {.cardx}
### 2) Choose a kernel and tail strategy
Pick a bulk kernel that matches the typical data regime, then decide whether tail modeling is needed.
- Overview: [Kernels](kernels.html)
- API: [Kernel support table](pkgdown/reference/kernel_support_table.html)
:::

::: {.cardx}
### 3) Fit, diagnose, iterate
Run MCMC, inspect mixing and stability, and validate predictive behavior before you interpret tail risk.
- Practical: [Cookbook](cookbook.html)
:::

::: {.cardx}
### 4) Prediction and summaries
Generate predictive quantities (densities, cdfs, quantiles, tail summaries) and extract results for reporting.
- Curated entry: [Reference](reference.html)
:::

::: {.cardx}
### 5) Conditional, causal, and survival workflows
When covariates or treatment effects matter, move to the conditional/causal toolchain.
- Start here: [Cookbook](cookbook.html)
:::

::: {.cardx}
### 6) Community + contribution
- Ideas and design: [Discussions](https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD/discussions)
- Bugs: [Issues](https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD/issues)
- Dev notes: [Developers](developers.html)
:::

:::

:::
:::
